results.question: My landlord hasn't returned my security deposit and it's been over 30 days since I moved out. What are my rights in Massachusetts?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlord must return security deposit within 30 days after move-out (Source 1, Source 4).
Landlord must provide an itemized receipt for any deductions (Source 1, Source 4).
Potential for triple damages if landlord violates G. L. c. 186, s.15B (Source 3).

results.question: Can my landlord charge me first, last, security deposit AND a broker fee when I move in? (MA)

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlords may charge first and last month's rent, security deposit, and lock fee (Source 5).
It is illegal for landlords to charge a broker's fee unless they are licensed realtors with a signed agreement (Source 3).
Landlords cannot charge up-front broker fees at the start of tenancy (Source 5).

results.question: My landlord entered my apartment without notice while I was at work. Is this legal in Massachusetts?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlords must provide at least one day's notice (24 hours) before entering a tenant's apartment, except in emergencies. (Source 1, Source 2)
The landlord may enter without approval only if there is a mechanical/repair emergency that could damage the whole building or if the tenant has abandoned the apartment. (Source 3)
Landlords should arrange a convenient time with the tenant to access the unit. (Source 1, Source 2)

results.question: How much notice does my landlord need to give me to end my month-to-month tenancy in MA?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A tenancy-at-will can be terminated by either party with 30 days' written notice or one full rental period, whichever is longer.
A lease for a specific term ends on its stated date; the landlord must provide proper notice before any changes.
The notice requirement applies to both landlords and tenants.

results.question: My apartment has mold and my landlord won't fix it. What can I do? (Boston)

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlords must provide safe and well-maintained apartments in compliance with the Massachusetts Sanitary Code (Source 1).
Tenants can file a complaint with the Inspectional Services Department if landlords aren't meeting housing codes (Source 4).
Contact the Inspectional Services Department for assistance with mold and unresponsive landlords (Source 3).

results.question: Can my landlord evict me in winter in Massachusetts?

per_fact_attribution.fact: There are no laws against evicting tenants during the winter season.
Tenants may request a 'Stay of Execution' for up to 6 months if an eviction is not their fault or they cannot find suitable housing.
Elderly tenants or those with disabilities may request a 'Stay of Execution' for up to a year.

results.question: My landlord is raising my rent by $500/month with only 2 weeks notice. Is this legal in MA?

per_fact_attribution.fact: MGL c.186 s.12 requires at least 30 days' notice for changes in rent for a tenancy at will.
The notice period must be equal to the interval between rent payments or 30 days, whichever is longer.
The landlord's notice of 2 weeks does not meet the legal requirement.

results.question: Can I withhold rent if my landlord won't make repairs in Massachusetts?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Tenants may withhold rent if the landlord fails to make necessary repairs (Source 1).
Consulting a lawyer or legal services is recommended before withholding rent (Source 1).
Landlords must maintain a habitable living condition, including heating, as per the State Sanitary Code (Source 3).

results.question: My landlord didn't put my security deposit in a separate bank account. Can I get triple damages?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Massachusetts law requires landlords to deposit security deposits in a separate interest-bearing account in a Massachusetts bank (MGL c. 186, s.15B).
Failure to comply with the requirement to place the deposit in a separate account entitles the tenant to immediate return of the deposit (Source 2).
In Castenholz v. Caira, the court ruled that if a landlord fails to place the deposit in an escrow account and the tenant must go to court to recover it, the tenant is entitled to triple damages.

results.question: I want to break my lease early in Massachusetts. What are the consequences?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A tenant with a lease is generally responsible for rent until the lease term ends.
If the landlord fails to maintain habitable conditions, the tenant may have grounds to break the lease and move out within a reasonable time.
The landlord has a duty to mitigate damages by trying to find a new tenant after the original tenant vacates.

results.question: My landlord is trying to charge me a late fee after 5 days. Is this allowed in MA?

per_fact_attribution.fact: There is no 'grace period' for payment of rent.
A late fee can only be charged if the rent is at least 30 days late.
This rule applies even if there is a written agreement allowing for late fees.

results.question: Can my landlord refuse to rent to me because I have kids? (Massachusetts)

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under Massachusetts fair housing law, refusing to rent to someone because they have children is unlawful discrimination.
Lead paint concerns do not justify refusing to rent to families with children; landlords must comply with deleading requirements.
Tenants who experience housing discrimination can file a complaint with the Boston Fair Housing Commission, MCAD, or HUD.

results.question: My landlord changed the locks while I was away for a week. Is this an illegal eviction in MA?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlords must follow the legal process and cannot force a tenant out by changing the locks or shutting off the utilities (Source 2).
A landlord cannot evict a tenant by physically removing the tenant or their personal property, or by changing the locks (Source 3).
To remove a tenant without the tenant's agreement, a landlord must get permission from the court (Source 3).

results.question: What is the eviction process in Massachusetts? How long does it take?

per_fact_attribution.fact: The eviction process starts with a notice to quit for non-payment of rent.
The landlord can file a summary process action in court under MGL c. 239.
After a court decision, the landlord must wait at least 10 days to obtain an execution for possession.

results.question: My landlord shut off my hot water to try to get me to move out. What can I do? (MA)

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under MGL c.186 s.14, a landlord cannot willfully shut off or fail to furnish hot water or other required utilities.
A tenant can only be required to pay for utilities if there are separate meters for each service.
Landlords who violate the quiet enjoyment statute may be liable for three months' rent or actual damages, whichever is greater.

results.question: Can my landlord make me pay for professional carpet cleaning when I move out in MA?

per_fact_attribution.fact: The lease must not include illegal terms such as requiring the tenant to pay the cost of repairing ordinary wear and tear. (Source 1)
Tenants are responsible for damage beyond normal wear and tear. (Source 1)
Professional carpet cleaning is typically considered maintenance for ordinary wear and tear.

results.question: I reported code violations and now my landlord is trying to evict me. Is this retaliation? (MA)

per_fact_attribution.fact: It is illegal for landlords to retaliate against tenants for engaging in protected activities, such as reporting conditions to the local board of health. [Source 2]
Retaliation can include starting an eviction proceeding. [Source 2]
Certain conduct by landlords will create a legal 'presumption' that the conduct was retaliatory if it occurs within six months of a tenant's protected activity. [Source 2]

results.question: What is the Section 8 housing program and how do I apply in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Section 8 provides rent subsidies for low-income families to afford housing (Source 1).
The Section 8 tenant-based voucher program waiting list is currently closed (Source 5).
Applicants can apply to public housing, Project-Based vouchers, and Mod Rehab programs if eligible (Source 5).

results.question: My landlord never gave me a statement of condition for my security deposit. Does this matter? (MA)

per_fact_attribution.fact: The landlord must provide a statement of condition within 10 days of the tenancy start or receipt of the security deposit (Source 1, Source 2).
The statement of condition describes the apartment's condition and any existing damage (Source 1, Source 2).
Failure to provide this document could affect claims on the security deposit (Source 1, Source 2).

results.question: Can my landlord charge me for bed bug treatment in Massachusetts?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlords must provide a habitable living environment, including pest control (Massachusetts Sanitary Code).
Tenants cannot be charged for extermination unless they caused the infestation.
The landlord's responsibility includes ensuring the property is free from pests.

results.question: How do I request a reasonable accommodation for my disability from my landlord in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Requests for reasonable accommodations must be made in writing, specifying the accommodation needed and the reasons (Source 2).
The BHA engages in an interactive process to review and decide upon accommodation requests (Source 1).
Forms for requesting accommodations can be obtained from various BHA offices or by contacting the Office of Civil Rights (Source 3).

results.question: My apartment doesn't have working smoke detectors. Is my landlord required to install them? (MA)

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under 105 CMR 410.330, the owner shall provide, install, and maintain smoke detectors and carbon monoxide alarms in every required residence.
Landlords are responsible for replacing or repairing non-functioning smoke detectors.
The local fire department and board of health enforce compliance with smoke detector requirements.

results.question: Can my landlord keep my security deposit for normal wear and tear in Massachusetts?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A landlord may only deduct from a security deposit for damage beyond normal wear and tear (Source 1).
The lease must not include terms requiring the tenant to pay for ordinary wear and tear (Source 3).
Landlords should not deduct for reasonable wear and tear when returning a security deposit (Source 4).

results.question: I'm a domestic violence survivor. Can I break my lease in Massachusetts?

per_fact_attribution.fact: MGL c.186 s.24 allows tenants to terminate a lease due to domestic violence.
Notification must be given within 3 months of the incident.
Landlords may request proof of the status as a victim.

results.question: What happens to my lease if my landlord sells the building in Massachusetts?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A tenant's lease remains in effect when property ownership changes; the new owner assumes the landlord's obligations.
The tenant should not lose their security deposit or last month's rent if the property is sold or a new management company takes over.

results.question: Can my landlord prohibit me from having a pet in Massachusetts?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlords may enforce a 'no pets' policy, but must make exceptions as a reasonable accommodation for tenants with disabilities who need service or emotional support animals.
In Massachusetts, there are no licensing or registration requirements for service animals or emotional support animals in housing.

results.question: My landlord is deducting my security deposit for things that were broken when I moved in. What can I do? (MA)

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlords must provide a 'statement of condition' within 10 days of tenancy start or upon receipt of the security deposit (Source 3).
Tenants have 15 days to add to or amend the 'statement of condition' (Source 3).
Landlords cannot deduct for damages listed in the 'statement of condition' (Source 5).

results.question: How do I file a complaint about housing code violations in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Complaints regarding housing violations must be addressed to the local health authority (Source 1).
The local health authority is responsible for conducting inspections and enforcement (Source 1).
In Boston, you can file a complaint if your landlord isn't meeting housing codes (Source 3).

results.question: Is my landlord required to provide heat in Massachusetts? What temperature?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlords must provide heat between September 15 and May 31 (Source 2).
The heating system must be in good operating condition and capable of heating every habitable room (105 CMR 410.160).
The landlord must pay for heat unless there is a written agreement stating otherwise (Source 2).

results.question: Can I sue my landlord in small claims court for not returning my security deposit in MA?

per_fact_attribution.fact: G. L. c. 186, s.15B requires landlords to return security deposits within 30 days.
Failure to comply with security deposit laws can result in triple damages (Castenholz v. Caira).
Security deposits must be held in an escrow account in a Massachusetts bank.

results.question: I just moved into a new apartment in Boston and my landlord didn't put my security deposit into a separate account. Is that allowed?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlords must hold security deposits in a separate interest-bearing account.
Landlords must provide notice to tenants about the security deposit account.
This requirement is specified in M.G.L. c. 186, § 15B.

results.question: My landlord stopped paying the electric bill, and I'm worried about losing service. What can I do according to Massachusetts law?

per_fact_attribution.fact: It is an unfair practice for a landlord to fail to provide required gas or electric services.
The landlord must pay gas or electric bills when due to prevent service loss.
Tenants are protected from losing services due to landlord's failure to pay utility bills.

results.question: Can my landlord charge me a pet fee in addition to my rent in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: It is illegal for you to charge a holding fee, pet fee, or broker or finder’s fee.
You can legally charge a tenant for first and last month’s rent, security deposit, and lock fee.

results.question: I'm renting an apartment in Boston and my lease is for 12 months. What are my rights regarding the security deposit my landlord is holding?

per_fact_attribution.fact: MGL c. 186, s.15B outlines the requirements for handling security deposits.
Landlords must deposit the security deposit in an interest-bearing account in a Massachusetts bank and provide the tenant with the bank details within 30 days.
Failure to properly handle the deposit can result in triple damages for the tenant.

results.question: I just found out my landlord is selling the building. What happens to my security deposit?

per_fact_attribution.fact: When a landlord sells a building, they must transfer the security deposit to the new owner.
The new owner has 45 days to send the tenant notice about the security deposit.
This requirement is specified in M.G.L. c. 186, § 15B.

results.question: Can my landlord in Boston legally require me to pay for the cost of repairing normal wear and tear when I move out?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A landlord cannot charge tenants for normal wear and tear repairs.
Deductions are only allowed for unpaid rent, unpaid increase in real estate taxes, and damages beyond normal wear and tear.
This is specified in M.G.L. c. 186, § 15B.

results.question: Can my landlord in Boston legally turn off my heat during the winter if I'm late on rent?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Massachusetts law requires landlords to provide essential services like heat.
Willfully failing to provide heat is a violation of MGL c.186 s.14.
Violations can lead to fines or imprisonment for the landlord.

results.question: Can my landlord in Boston evict me because I reported a building code violation to the city?

per_fact_attribution.fact: MGL c.186 s.18 prohibits reprisals against tenants for reporting building code violations.
Retaliatory eviction is a common defense against eviction under Massachusetts law.
Landlords must follow a formal eviction process and cannot evict tenants for protected activities.

results.question: Can my landlord evict me because I reported a health violation to the city?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under MGL c.239 s.2A, it is a defense to eviction that the action was taken in reprisal for the tenant reporting code violations or exercising legal rights.
A landlord cannot evict a tenant by physically removing them or changing the locks; they must go through the court process.

results.question: I'm facing eviction in Boston and I have kids in public school. Are there any programs that can help us?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Access to Counsel Program provides legal representation for families with children in K-12 schools to prevent evictions.
The program is a partnership between the Mayor's Office of Housing, Boston Public Schools, Family Aid Boston, and Greater Boston Legal Services.
This service is available only in Boston.

results.question: What steps should I take to prepare for an eviction court hearing in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A landlord must file a case in court and obtain a summary process judgment to evict a tenant.
Tenants have the right to respond to an eviction case, present defenses and counterclaims, and bring documents and witnesses to trial.
If a tenant does not file an answer or appear, the court may enter a default judgment against them.

results.question: Can I withhold rent if my apartment in Boston has serious code violations?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Massachusetts law allows tenants to withhold rent when there are housing code violations that endanger health or safety.
Under MGL c.239 s.8A, a tenant can claim the difference between the agreed rent and the fair value of the premises with defects.
The landlord must have knowledge of the conditions and fail to take reasonable steps to correct them.

results.question: Can I withhold rent in Boston if my landlord hasn't fixed the heating in my apartment?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Tenants must notify the landlord of housing code violations and give reasonable time to repair before withholding rent.
Rent withholding is permissible when conditions violate the sanitary code and impair health or safety.
The tenant should document the conditions and keep records of all communications with the landlord.

results.question: I just got an eviction notice in Boston. When do I need to appear in court, and what happens if I don't respond in time?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Tenants must file an answer with the court before the answer date on the summons.
Tenants can raise defenses and counterclaims in response to an eviction.
If a tenant fails to file an answer or appear, the court may enter a default judgment and order eviction.

results.question: I'm struggling to pay my rent in a Boston public housing unit. Is there any way I can get an exemption or reduction due to my financial situation?

per_fact_attribution.fact: MGL c.121B s.32 outlines the conditions under which a tenant can be exempt from the minimum rent due to severe financial hardship.
The exemption can be applied if the tenant is awaiting eligibility for assistance programs or has experienced a significant change in income.
The housing authority is responsible for determining eligibility for the hardship exemption.

results.question: Can my landlord increase my rent during a market emergency in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: 940 CMR 3.17 makes it an unfair or deceptive practice for a landlord to rent a unit with conditions that violate law or endanger health and safety.
Landlords are prohibited from failing to maintain dwelling units in compliance with the sanitary code.
The regulation covers conditions at the inception of tenancy and ongoing maintenance obligations.

results.question: Can my landlord enter my apartment without notice to make repairs?

per_fact_attribution.fact: The landlord should provide at least one day’s notice (24 hours) and attempt to arrange a convenient time to access the unit.
Unless there is an emergency, the landlord should never enter your apartment without advance notice and your permission.

results.question: My apartment in Boston has been unbearably hot this winter, constantly over 78°F. Is my landlord violating any regulations?

per_fact_attribution.fact: 105 CMR 410.180 states the heating system must maintain at least 68F between 7AM-11PM and 64F between 11PM-7AM during heating season.
The heating system shall not cause the temperature to exceed 78F in any room (105 CMR 410.180(B)).
Temperature is measured at a height of five feet above floor level.

results.question: Can my landlord in Boston be penalized if they don't fix a serious health code violation in my apartment after being ordered to?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under 105 CMR 410.920, any person who fails to comply with an order shall be fined not less than $10 nor more than $500 per violation.
Each day's failure to comply with an order constitutes a separate violation.
The board of health can take enforcement action including ordering corrections and assessing penalties.

results.question: Can my landlord block the hallway with furniture in our apartment building in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under 105 CMR 410.260, every dwelling unit shall have means of exit allowing safe passage in accordance with the building code.
No person shall cause obstruction of any means of egress or passageway.
The owner shall maintain free from obstruction every means of egress in common areas used by occupants of more than one dwelling unit.

results.question: My landlord mentioned my building might have asbestos. What are they required to do about it?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under 105 CMR 410.250, the owner shall maintain all asbestos-containing materials in intact condition.
The sanitary code defines asbestos-containing material and requires owners to ensure it does not release fibers.
Occupants must maintain appliances and facilities in sanitary condition and report hazardous conditions to the owner.

results.question: My landlord in Boston is trying to keep my entire security deposit after I accidentally damaged a wall. Is this allowed?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A landlord can only deduct from a security deposit for damage beyond normal wear and tear.
The landlord must provide an itemized list of damages with cost estimates within 30 days after the tenancy ends.
If the landlord improperly withholds the deposit, the tenant may be entitled to triple damages under MGL c.186 s.15B.

results.question: Can my landlord charge me an exorbitant fee for emergency repairs during a declared state of emergency in Massachusetts?

per_fact_attribution.fact: 940 CMR 3.18 prohibits charging unconscionably high prices during a declared state of emergency.
A price is unconscionable if there is a gross disparity with pre-emergency prices or other businesses' prices.
The disparity must not be substantially attributable to increased supplier prices or abnormal market disruptions.

results.question: Can my landlord enter my apartment without my permission in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: The landlord should provide at least one day's notice (24 hours) before entering a tenant's apartment.
A landlord may enter to inspect the premises, make repairs, or show the unit to prospective tenants or purchasers.
Unless there is an emergency, the landlord should never enter without advance notice and the tenant's permission.

results.question: Can my landlord in Boston charge me a late fee if I'm only a few days late on rent?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A late fee can only be charged if there is a written agreement that allows it.
Even with a written agreement, the late fee cannot be charged unless rent is at least 30 days late.
There is no grace period for payment of rent; rent is due on the date specified in the tenancy terms.

results.question: Can my landlord shut off my electricity if I’m behind on rent payments in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under MGL c.186 s.14, a landlord who is required to furnish utilities may not willfully fail to furnish them.
A landlord who violates this section may be liable for actual and consequential damages or three months' rent, whichever is greater.
The tenant may also recover costs and attorney's fees for such violations.

results.question: Can my landlord shut off my water if I am behind on rent?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under MGL c.186 s.14, it is unlawful for a landlord to willfully shut off or fail to provide water service.
Interference with utilities constitutes a violation of the tenant's right to quiet enjoyment.
Tenants can seek damages including three months' rent or actual damages, whichever is greater.

results.question: Can my landlord shut off my water if I am behind on rent in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under MGL c.186 s.14, a landlord required to furnish water cannot willfully fail to provide it.
Shutting off water to force a tenant out is prohibited and constitutes an unfair practice.
The tenant may recover damages of three months' rent or actual damages, whichever is greater, plus attorney's fees.

results.question: Can my landlord in Boston deny me housing because of my religion?

per_fact_attribution.fact: MGL c.151B s.4 prohibits refusing to rent or lease housing because of a person's religious creed.
Unlawful discrimination in housing includes factors such as race, color, religious creed, national origin, sex, and other protected classes.
Violations can be reported to the Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination (MCAD).

results.question: Can my landlord in Boston refuse to rent to me because I have children?

per_fact_attribution.fact: MGL c.151B s.4 prohibits refusing to rent or lease housing because a person has children.
Housing discrimination based on familial status (having children) is unlawful under Massachusetts and federal fair housing laws.
Tenants who experience discrimination can file a complaint with MCAD or HUD.

results.question: Is my landlord required to post any notices about tenant rights in the building?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Massachusetts fair housing law prohibits discrimination in housing based on multiple protected classes.
Landlords of covered housing must comply with fair housing requirements including posting notices.
The Boston Fair Housing Commission and MCAD enforce anti-discrimination laws in housing.

results.question: Can my landlord refuse to rent to me because I have a service dog?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Massachusetts law prohibits housing discrimination based on the need for a service dog.
MGL c.151B s.4 outlines unlawful housing discrimination practices.

results.question: I heard Boston has some new rules about affordable housing requirements for developers. Can someone explain what these are?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Boston's Inclusionary Zoning requires market-rate developments with seven or more units to create income-restricted housing at 15 to 17 percent of units.
Large rental projects must additionally provide 3 percent of units for voucher holders.
Developers may contribute to the Inclusionary Development Policy Fund in lieu of building income-restricted units on site.

results.question: I'm a renter in Boston and my landlord wants to switch my lease to a month-to-month agreement. What are the pros and cons of this change?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A tenancy-at-will agreement offers more flexibility, but a lease offers more security.
You can offer a tenant a lease, which typically runs for one year, or a verbal or written tenancy-at-will agreement, which runs from month to month.

results.question: Can my landlord make me pay a broker fee if I'm renting an apartment in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A new law went into effect on August 1, 2025, prohibiting brokers from charging fees to tenants when they primarily provide services to the landlord.
A landlord may not charge a tenant or prospective tenant a fee for services that a broker provided to the landlord.

results.question: I heard my apartment building is being foreclosed. I have a lease with a rental subsidy. What happens to my lease after foreclosure?

per_fact_attribution.fact: MGL c.186 s.13A states that foreclosure does not affect the tenancy agreement of a tenant whose rental payment is subsidized under state or federal law.
The foreclosing entity must assume the lease and rental subsidy contract.
Your tenancy will be considered as a tenant at will upon foreclosure.

results.question: I'm renting an apartment in Boston with lead paint, and I'm concerned about safety for my child. How can I get help to address this issue?

per_fact_attribution.fact: The Lead Safe Boston program offers financial help for lead removal in homes.
The program includes a lead inspection to assess needed work and costs.
Assistance is provided to find a contractor and set up a project timeline.

results.question: I'm renting an apartment in Boston and concerned about lead paint. How can I get help to remove it?

per_fact_attribution.fact: The Lead Safe Boston program assists with lead inspections and finding contractors.
Applications are available in multiple languages.
The program helps set up a project timeline for lead removal.

results.question: I'm renting an apartment in Boston with peeling paint, and I suspect it has lead. How can I address this issue?

per_fact_attribution.fact: The Lead Safe Boston program helps cover costs for lead removal.
A lead inspection will determine the extent of work needed.
The program assists in finding a contractor to handle the lead removal project.

results.question: I'm renting an apartment in Boston and my landlord says I have to pay for my own heat. Is that legal?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A tenant can only be required to pay for utilities if there are separate meters for each service charged.
Landlords must pay for heat unless a written agreement states otherwise.
Landlords who pay for heat must provide it between September 15 and May 31.

results.question: I'm renting an apartment in Boston and my landlord wants me to pay for heat and hot water. Is this allowed?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A tenant can only be required to pay for utilities if there are separate meters for each service charged.
The landlord must pay for heat and hot water unless the tenant has signed a written agreement to pay.
Landlords who pay for heat must provide it between September 15 and May 31.

results.question: Can my landlord in Boston make me pay for heat if there's no separate meter?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A tenant can only be required to pay for utilities if there are separate meters for each service charged.
Your landlord must pay for heat and hot water unless you have signed a written agreement that says you must pay.
Landlords who pay for heat must provide it between September 15 and May 31.

results.question: Can I ask for a modification to my lease terms due to my disability if I'm struggling to meet certain requirements?

per_fact_attribution.fact: An 'otherwise qualified' individual with a disability can meet program eligibility and requirements with or without a reasonable accommodation.
Clients may request BHA to consider their disability and work with them if they're struggling with program obligations due to their disability.

results.question: I'm a single applicant looking for housing in Boston. Can I choose between a studio and a 1-bedroom unit?

per_fact_attribution.fact: New admissions single individuals are assigned to whichever unit size, studio or 1-bedroom, is first available.
Applicants must document why they must reside in a 1-bedroom unit if they have a preference.
The BHA intends to implement an option for single individuals to choose a waiting list for either a studio or 1-bedroom.

results.question: I requested a specific accommodation for my disability from the Boston Housing Authority, but they offered me a different one. Can they do that?

per_fact_attribution.fact: If your request is not reasonable, the BHA will propose an accommodation that tries to meet your needs as effectively as possible.
If your request is reasonable, but there is a less burdensome but equally effective accommodation available, the BHA may offer you that accommodation instead.

results.question: I'm on the waiting list for a BHA apartment in Boston. Can I change the development choices I've selected?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Applicants may add or remove developments choices at any time prior to entering the final eligibility screening process.
Once the applicant is contacted by the BHA to schedule the personal interview appointment no changes in development choice(s) shall be accepted.
Applicants are encouraged to visit the public housing developments before applying or before entering the final screening process.

results.question: Is my landlord required to provide a refrigerator in my Boston apartment?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlords are not required to provide refrigerators, but if they offer them, they must maintain them.
Under 105 CMR 410, a refrigerator with freezer is only required if specified in a written rental agreement.

results.question: Do I need to have renters insurance as a tenant in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Renters insurance is not legally required in Massachusetts.
The City of Boston recommends renters insurance to cover damages or losses from fire or other disasters.
The landlord is generally not responsible for fire damage to tenant's belongings absent negligence.

results.question: Do I need to get renters insurance for my apartment in Boston?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Renters insurance is not legally required by Massachusetts law.
Renters insurance can cover damage or loss to personal belongings from events like fire.
Without insurance, tenants bear the cost of replacing belongings damaged by fire or disaster.

results.question: My landlord never put my $2,000 security deposit in a separate bank account, and now that I've moved out 45 days ago, they still haven't returned it. What legal steps can I take and what damages can I recover?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlord must place security deposit in a separate interest-bearing bank account protected from creditors.
Landlord must return security deposit within 30 days after tenancy ends.
Tenant should send a demand letter first via certified mail, return receipt requested.
If landlord fails to properly deposit or return, court must award 3 times the security deposit amount.
Tenant can also recover interest, court costs, and attorney's fees.
Can file in Small Claims court for up to $7,000; 3x statutory damages may exceed $7,000.

results.question: I have a 4-year-old child and discovered peeling paint in our pre-1978 apartment. I reported it to my landlord and the Board of Health. Now my landlord served me with an eviction notice. Is this legal, and what can I do?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Massachusetts Lead Law requires landlords to remove or cover lead paint hazards in pre-1978 homes where a child under 6 lives.
Landlord cannot refuse to rent to or evict a tenant because they have children or because of lead paint.
It is illegal for a landlord to retaliate for reporting a lead paint violation to the Board of Health under M.G.L. c. 186 § 18.
Tenant can raise retaliation as both a defense and counterclaim in eviction proceedings.
If court finds retaliation, landlord may be ordered to pay 1-3 months' rent in damages.

results.question: My apartment has had no heat since December even though my landlord is responsible for it, and I've told them multiple times in writing. I started withholding rent and now they're trying to evict me for nonpayment. What are my legal rights?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Landlord must maintain minimum heat of 68°F (7am-11pm) and 64°F (11pm-7am) during heating season (Sept 15 - June 15).
Failure to maintain habitable conditions after notice is an unfair/deceptive practice under 940 CMR 3.17.
Tenant may withhold rent for serious code violations but should put withheld rent in a separate bank account.
Bad conditions are a defense to nonpayment eviction if conditions violate Sanitary Code and landlord had knowledge.
A judge can determine fair rental value of the apartment in its defective condition and calculate how much rent is actually owed.
Tenant may also have a counterclaim for damages based on the decreased value of the apartment.

results.question: My landlord is responsible for gas service per our lease, but they stopped paying the bill. The gas company sent a shutoff notice. What are my rights, and can I deduct utility payments from my rent?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under 940 CMR 3.17, it is an unfair practice for a landlord obligated to provide gas/electric to fail to provide service or expose tenants to risk of loss by failing to pay bills.
Utility company must give tenants at least 30 days' notice before shutting off service when landlord fails to pay.
Tenants can band together to pay a projected bill to avoid shutoff and deduct those payments from rent.
Tenants are protected from retaliation for withholding rent for utility payments.
Failure to provide utilities violates quiet enjoyment — tenants can win up to 3 months' rent and attorney's fees.

results.question: I came home and found my landlord changed the locks on my apartment. My belongings are still inside. What are my legal options and what damages can I recover?

per_fact_attribution.fact: A landlord cannot lock out a tenant or remove belongings without a court order.
Under M.G.L. c. 186 § 15F, tenant can recover 3 months' rent or 3 times actual damages, plus attorney's fees.
Any lease provision waiving the tenant's right to claim constructive eviction is void as against public policy.
Lockout is a violation of quiet enjoyment and can be raised as a defense/counterclaim in eviction.
Willful violation of M.G.L. c. 186 § 14 is an unfair practice under 940 CMR 3.17.

results.question: I'm being evicted for nonpayment of rent. My landlord never gave me a statement of condition when I moved in, never paid me interest on my security deposit, and I never got a receipt. Can I use any of this in court to fight the eviction?

per_fact_attribution.fact: If landlord failed to provide receipt, statement of condition, separate bank account, or yearly interest, tenant has a defense to eviction.
Security deposit violations can be raised as both a defense and a counterclaim in nonpayment evictions.
As a counterclaim, tenant may be entitled to 3 times the security deposit and interest owed.
If tenant wins counterclaim but still owes rent, court gives 7 days to pay the difference to keep the apartment.
Landlord cannot claim tenant caused damage if they didn't provide an itemized list within 30 days.

results.question: My building was just foreclosed on by the bank. The new owner says I need to leave within 30 days. I've lived here for 5 years with a lease. What are my rights?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under M.G.L. c. 186A, a foreclosing owner can only evict a tenant with just cause (nonpayment, material violation, nuisance, illegal activity, refusal of access).
A bona fide lease must be honored — tenant cannot be evicted simply because of foreclosure.
Tenant can request a stay of eviction for up to 6 months (or 12 months if elderly/disabled) if tenancy terminated without fault.
To get a stay, tenant must show they cannot find suitable alternative housing and have made reasonable effort.

results.question: My landlord told me he doesn't want to rent to families with children and is now trying to evict me after I had a baby. Where can I file a complaint and can I fight the eviction?

per_fact_attribution.fact: In Boston, it is illegal to discriminate in housing based on family status.
Tenant can file a complaint with Boston Fair Housing and Equity at 617-635-2500 or via online intake form.
Discrimination is a defense and counterclaim in nonpayment, fault, and no-fault eviction proceedings.
Refusing to rent to or evicting someone because they have children is illegal under state and federal fair housing law.

results.question: My landlord has refused to fix a leaking roof and mold problem for 6 months despite my written requests. I've called the Board of Health and they issued a repair order, but my landlord still hasn't fixed it. What court actions can I take?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Tenant can file a Tenant Petition asking a judge to order landlord to repair Sanitary Code violations.
A judge can order repairs, appoint a receiver (temporary landlord) to make repairs, or lower rent to fair rental value.
Tenant can file a civil complaint seeking money damages for harm suffered from bad conditions.
Failure to repair after notice is an unfair/deceptive practice under 940 CMR 3.17.
Under M.G.L. c. 93A, tenant can recover double or treble damages plus attorney's fees for unfair practices.

results.question: My landlord just told me he's converting our building to condominiums and is raising my rent by 50%. He also said the new condo owner won't be responsible for my security deposit. Is any of this legal?

per_fact_attribution.fact: Under condo conversion law, rent cannot increase by more than 10% per year or above the CPI increase, whichever is less.
If tenant has a lease, landlord cannot change rent until the lease ends (except through a tax escalator clause).
When ownership changes, the new owner becomes responsible for last month's rent and security deposit paid to the former landlord.
This is true even if the former landlord did not transfer the deposit to the new owner.
The owner must have only an 'intent to convert' for tenant protections to apply.
